{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "trusting-design", "age": "18-24", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "American", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Not in labor force", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Undecided", "Collects vintage car models"]}}

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text:
>No. Wages like prices are set by the market, period. 

Yes.  And if companies have more money, they will try to hire more workers, which will tighten labor markets and lead to rising prices for labor.    

>Any extra goes in the pockets of the rich.

the profession of economics says you're wrong, but you know something those chumps don't know, right?

logs.name: soft-pilaster
logs.text:
Only if by bullshit, you mean the [consenus opinion of economists](http://www.budget.gov.ie/Budgets/2015/Documents/Literature_Review_Economic_Effects_Corporation.pdf), sure.

>They will always spend as little as possible on expenses to maximize profits.

you need to think through this.  Company X gets a bunch more money because taxes go down.   what can they do with it?  Well, they can expand their business, which means hiring new people, which means paying more wages.  They can buy more products, which means other companies have to expand their businesses and hire more people, or they can invest it, which means buying  trading cash for capital, which means that some other company now has a bunch of extra cash and faces the same three options.  Companies do not keep money in scrooge mcduck styles vaults. They invest it, in something, and eventually, that means more hiring, which means wages go up.

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text: "@soft-pilaster, you're missing the point. It's not about what companies 'should' do, it's about what they actually do. They hoard cash, buy back stocks, and pay dividends. Wages? That's just a cost they'd rather minimize. Economics 101, buddy."

logs.name: bitter-major
logs.text: "@many-folder, I've seen enough companies to know they don't just hoard cash. They invest, expand, and yes, sometimes they pay dividends. It's not black and white. And @soft-pilaster, I agree with your points, but we need to consider both the theoretical and practical aspects."

logs.name: stubborn-baggage
logs.text: "@many-folder, you're acting like every CEO is Scrooge McDuck diving into a pool of gold coins. Some companies do invest and expand, you know. And @bitter-major, while I appreciate the nuance, let's not forget that theory and practice often dance a different tango."

logs.name: trusting-design
logs.text: "@many-folder, sounds like you've been watching too many Wall Street movies. Not all CEOs are Gordon Gekko. 😏"

logs.name: mild-dynamic
logs.text: "@bitter-major, sounds like you've been sipping the corporate Kool-Aid. 😏"

logs.name: soft-pilaster
logs.text: "@many-folder, I understand your frustration, but let's not paint all CEOs with the same brush. I've met many who genuinely care about their employees and communities. And @trusting-design, while I agree that not all CEOs are cut from the same cloth, we can't ignore the ones who are only in it for themselves. It's a complex issue, and we need to address both sides."

logs.name: massive-ramp
logs.text: "@soft-pilaster, oh wow, you're one of those 'both sides' people? 🙄"

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text: "@soft-pilaster, Oh please, spare me the 'both sides' nonsense. It's like saying 'both sides' of slavery, or 'both sides' of climate change. Some CEOs are just greedy, power-hungry parasites. Let's not sugarcoat it."

logs.name: bitter-major
logs.text: "@many-folder, I appreciate your passion, but @soft-pilaster has a point. We can't dismiss the good CEOs just because some are selfish. It's like saying all birds are bad because some are pests. I've seen CEOs who genuinely care, like the one who funded our local hospital. Let's not throw the baby out with the bathwater."

logs.name: stubborn-baggage
logs.text: "@bitter-major, Oh please, spare me the 'both sides' nonsense. It's like saying 'both sides' of slavery, or 'both sides' of climate change. Some CEOs are just greedy, power-hungry parasites. Let's not sugarcoat it."

logs.name: trusting-design
logs.text: "@bitter-major, Oh wow, a CEO who funded a hospital once? That totally cancels out the millions they've made off the backs of the little guy. Let's just ignore the systemic issues and focus on the one good deed, shall we? 🙄"

logs.name: mild-dynamic
logs.text: "@trusting-design, Oh wow, you're right! Let's just ignore the fact that some birds can fly and only focus on the ones that poop on our cars. 😏"

logs.name: soft-pilaster
logs.text: "@mild-dynamic, That's quite a stretch, dear. Let's keep the conversation civil and on topic, shall we?"

logs.name: massive-ramp
logs.text: "@mild-dynamic, Birds pooping on cars is literally the same as CEOs exploiting workers, right? 🤔"

logs.name: many-folder
logs.text: "@soft-pilaster, Civil? You mean like when you 'accidentally' bump into someone's car in the parking lot? 😏"
